name: Euler's Identity
author: Leonhard Euler
description: Considered the most beautiful equation in mathematics, it connects five fundamental constants.

name: Mass-Energy Equivalence
author: Albert Einstein
description: States that mass can be converted into energy and vice versa, forming the basis of nuclear physics.

name: Law of Universal Gravitation
author: Isaac Newton
description: Describes the attractive force between two bodies with mass, explaining both falling apples and planetary orbits.

name: Schrödinger Equation (Time-Dependent)
author: Erwin Schrödinger
description: A linear partial differential equation that governs the wave function of a quantum-mechanical system.

name: Pythagorean Theorem
author: Pythagoras of Samos
description: A fundamental relation in Euclidean geometry among the three sides of a right triangle.

name: Fourier Transform
author: Joseph Fourier
description: Decomposes a function of time into the frequencies that make it up, essential in signal processing.

name: Heisenberg Uncertainty Principle
author: Werner Heisenberg
description: Asserts a fundamental limit to the precision with which certain pairs of physical properties can be known.

name: Quadratic Formula
author: Brahmagupta
description: Provides the solution(s) to a quadratic equation, finding the roots of a parabola.

name: Navier-Stokes Equation (Incompressible)
author: Claude-Louis Navier & George Stokes
description: Describes the motion of viscous fluid substances, foundational for aerodynamics and weather forecasting.

name: Planck-Einstein Relation
author: Max Planck & Albert Einstein
description: States that the energy of a photon is proportional to its frequency, a foundational step in quantum mechanics.

name: Bayes' Theorem
author: Thomas Bayes
description: Describes the probability of an event, based on prior knowledge of conditions that might be related to the event. Foundational for statistics and machine learning.

name: Euler's Polyhedral Formula
author: Leonhard Euler
description: Relates the number of vertices, edges, and faces of any convex polyhedron. A cornerstone of topology.

name: Shannon Entropy
author: Claude Shannon
description: Calculates the minimum average amount of data required to encode a string of symbols, founding the field of information theory.

name: Boltzmann Entropy Formula
author: Ludwig Boltzmann
description: Expresses the entropy of a system in terms of the number of its possible microscopic states. It bridges the gap between the microscopic and macroscopic world.

name: De Broglie Wavelength
author: Louis de Broglie
description: Shows that any moving particle or object has an associated wave. A central concept of quantum mechanics.

name: Ideal Gas Law
author: Benoît Paul Émile Clapeyron
description: The equation of state of a hypothetical ideal gas, combining several empirical gas laws.

name: Snell's Law
author: Willebrord Snellius
description: Describes the relationship between the angles of incidence and refraction, when referring to light or other waves passing through a boundary between two different media.

name: Hooke's Law
author: Robert Hooke
description: States that the force needed to extend or compress a spring by some distance scales linearly with respect to that distance.

name: Gauss's Law for Electricity
author: Carl Friedrich Gauss
description: One of Maxwell's four equations. It relates the distribution of electric charge to the resulting electric field.

name: Logistic Growth Equation
author: Pierre François Verhulst
description: Models population growth where the rate of reproduction is proportional to both the existing population and the amount of available resources.

name: Einstein Field Equations
author: Albert Einstein
description: The fundamental equations of general relativity, describing how mass and energy warp spacetime.

name: Dirac Equation
author: Paul Dirac
description: A relativistic wave equation that describes all spin-1/2 massive particles and predicted the existence of antimatter.

name: Fermat's Last Theorem
author: Pierre de Fermat
description: States that no three positive integers a, b, and c satisfy the equation for any integer value of n greater than 2.

name: Drake Equation
author: Frank Drake
description: A probabilistic argument used to estimate the number of active, communicative extraterrestrial civilizations in the Milky Way.
latex: N = R_* \cdot f_p \cdot n_e \cdot f_l \cdot f_i \cdot f_c \cdot L

name: Black-Scholes Equation
author: Fischer Black & Myron Scholes
description: A partial differential equation governing the price evolution of options, foundational in modern financial theory.
latex: \frac{\partial V}{\partial t} + \frac{1}{2}\sigma^2 S^2 \frac{\partial^2 V}{\partial S^2} + rS\frac{\partial V}{\partial S} - rV = 0

name: Wave Equation
author: Jean le Rond d'Alembert
description: A second-order linear partial differential equation for the description of waves, such as sound, light, or water waves.

name: Heat Equation
author: Joseph Fourier
description: Describes the distribution of heat (or variation in temperature) in a given region over time.

name: Second Law of Thermodynamics
author: Rudolf Clausius
description: States that the total entropy of an isolated system can never decrease over time.

name: Coulomb's Law
author: Charles-Augustin de Coulomb
description: Quantifies the amount of force between two stationary, electrically charged particles.

name: Lorentz Force Law
author: Hendrik Lorentz
description: The combination of electric and magnetic force on a point charge due to electromagnetic fields.

name: Hubble's Law
author: Edwin Hubble
description: Observation that galaxies are moving away from Earth at speeds proportional to their distance, implying the expansion of the universe.

name: Kepler's Third Law
author: Johannes Kepler
description: The square of a planet's orbital period is proportional to the cube of the semi-major axis of its orbit.

name: Stefan-Boltzmann Law
author: Josef Stefan & Ludwig Boltzmann
description: Describes the power radiated from a black body in terms of its temperature.

name: Bernoulli's Equation
author: Daniel Bernoulli
description: Relates the pressure, velocity, and height of a flowing fluid, based on the principle of conservation of energy.

name: Ohm's Law
author: Georg Ohm
description: States that the current through a conductor is proportional to the voltage across it.

name: Faraday's Law of Induction
author: Michael Faraday
description: Predicts how a magnetic field will interact with an electric circuit to produce an electromotive force.

name: Ampère-Maxwell Law
author: James Clerk Maxwell
description: Relates the integrated magnetic field around a closed loop to the electric current passing through the loop.

name: Gibbs Free Energy
author: Josiah Willard Gibbs
description: A thermodynamic potential that can be used to calculate the maximum reversible work that may be performed by a thermodynamic system.

name: Arrhenius Equation
author: Svante Arrhenius
description: A formula for the temperature dependence of reaction rates in physical chemistry.

name: Nernst Equation
author: Walther Nernst
description: Relates the reduction potential of an electrochemical reaction to the standard electrode potential, temperature, and activities of the chemical species.

name: Henderson-Hasselbalch Equation
author: Lawrence Joseph Henderson & Karl Albert Hasselbalch
description: Describes the derivation of pH as a measure of acidity in biological and chemical systems.

name: Rydberg Formula
author: Johannes Rydberg
description: Used in atomic physics to describe the wavelengths of spectral lines of many chemical elements.

name: Bragg's Law
author: William Lawrence Bragg
description: Gives the angles for coherent and incoherent scattering from a crystal lattice.

name: Compton Scattering
author: Arthur Compton
description: Describes the decrease in energy (increase in wavelength) of an X-ray or gamma ray photon, when it interacts with matter.

name: Time Dilation
author: Albert Einstein
description: The difference in the elapsed time measured by two clocks, due to a relative velocity between them.

name: Length Contraction
author: Hendrik Lorentz & Albert Einstein
description: The phenomenon that a moving object's length is measured to be shorter than its proper length.

name: Lorentz Factor
author: Hendrik Lorentz
description: The factor by which time, length, and relativistic mass change for an object while that object is moving.

name: Newton's Second Law of Motion
author: Isaac Newton
description: The rate of change of momentum of a body over time is directly proportional to the force applied.

name: Kinetic Energy
author: Gaspard-Gustave de Coriolis
description: The energy that an object possesses due to its motion.

name: Gravitational Potential Energy
author: Isaac Newton
description: The energy an object possesses because of its position in a gravitational field.

name: Centripetal Force
author: Christiaan Huygens
description: The force that makes a body follow a curved path.

name: Taylor Series
author: Brook Taylor
description: A representation of a function as an infinite sum of terms that are calculated from the values of the function's derivatives at a single point.

name: Binomial Theorem
author: Isaac Newton
description: Describes the algebraic expansion of powers of a binomial.

name: Laplace's Equation
author: Pierre-Simon Laplace
description: A second-order partial differential equation useful in electromagnetism, astronomy, and fluid dynamics.

name: Poisson's Equation
author: Siméon Denis Poisson
description: A broad-utility partial differential equation used in electrostatics, mechanical engineering and theoretical physics.

name: Cauchy-Riemann Equations
author: Augustin-Louis Cauchy & Bernhard Riemann
description: A system of two partial differential equations which form a necessary and sufficient condition for a complex function to be holomorphic.
latex: \frac{\partial u}{\partial x} = \frac{\partial v}{\partial y} \quad \text{and} \quad \frac{\partial u}{\partial y} = -\frac{\partial v}{\partial x}

name: First Law of Thermodynamics
author: Rudolf Clausius
description: A version of the law of conservation of energy, adapted for thermodynamic processes.

name: Kirchhoff's Current Law
author: Gustav Kirchhoff
description: States that the algebraic sum of currents in a network of conductors meeting at a point is zero.

name: Work-Energy Theorem
author: Gaspard-Gustave de Coriolis
description: States that the net work done by the forces on an object equals the change in its kinetic energy.

name: Gauss's Law for Magnetism
author: Carl Friedrich Gauss
description: One of Maxwell's equations. It states that the magnetic field B has divergence equal to zero, implying there are no magnetic monopoles.

name: Biot-Savart Law
author: Jean-Baptiste Biot & Félix Savart
description: Describes the magnetic field generated by a steady electric current.

name: Tsiolkovsky Rocket Equation
author: Konstantin Tsiolkovsky
description: Calculates the maximum change of velocity of a vehicle that follows the basic principle of a rocket.

name: Euler-Lagrange Equation
author: Leonhard Euler & Joseph-Louis Lagrange
description: The fundamental equation of calculus of variations, heavily used in classical mechanics.

name: Hamilton's Equations
author: William Rowan Hamilton
description: A reformulation of classical mechanics using momenta and coordinates.

name: Klein-Gordon Equation
author: Oskar Klein & Walter Gordon
description: A relativistic version of the Schrödinger equation for spinless particles.

name: Fick's First Law of Diffusion
author: Adolf Fick
description: Relates the diffusive flux to the concentration under the assumption of steady state.

name: Michaelis-Menten Equation
author: Leonor Michaelis & Maud Menten
description: One of the best-known models of enzyme kinetics in biochemistry.

name: Lotka-Volterra Equations (Prey)
author: Alfred J. Lotka & Vito Volterra
description: A pair of first-order nonlinear differential equations describing the dynamics of biological systems in which two species interact.

name: Hardy-Weinberg Principle
author: G. H. Hardy & Wilhelm Weinberg
description: States that allele and genotype frequencies in a population will remain constant from generation to generation in the absence of other evolutionary influences.

name: Newton's Law of Cooling
author: Isaac Newton
description: States that the rate of heat loss of a body is directly proportional to the difference in the temperatures between the body and its surroundings.

name: Fourier's Law of Heat Conduction
author: Joseph Fourier
description: States that the time rate of heat transfer through a material is proportional to the negative gradient in the temperature.

name: Darcy's Law
author: Henry Darcy
description: Describes the flow of a fluid through a porous medium.

name: Stokes' Law
author: George Gabriel Stokes
description: Calculates the frictional force acting on spherical objects with very small Reynolds numbers in a viscous fluid.

name: Reynolds Number
author: Osborne Reynolds
description: A dimensionless quantity that helps predict fluid flow patterns in different situations.

name: Euler's Formula for Complex Numbers
author: Leonhard Euler
description: Establishes the fundamental relationship between the trigonometric functions and the complex exponential function.

name: De Moivre's Formula
author: Abraham de Moivre
description: Connects complex numbers and trigonometry.

name: Stirling's Approximation
author: James Stirling
description: An approximation for factorials, extremely useful in applied mathematics and statistical mechanics.

name: Riemann Zeta Function
author: Bernhard Riemann
description: A function of a complex variable that analytically continues the sum of the Dirichlet series, central to number theory.

name: Prime Number Theorem
author: Jacques Hadamard & Charles Jean de la Vallée Poussin
description: Describes the asymptotic distribution of the prime numbers among the positive integers.

name: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
author: Isaac Newton & Gottfried Wilhelm Leibniz
description: Links the concept of differentiating a function with the concept of integrating a function.

name: Larmor Formula
author: Joseph Larmor
description: Calculates the total power radiated by a non-relativistic point charge as it accelerates.

name: Wien's Displacement Law
author: Wilhelm Wien
description: States that the black-body radiation curve for different temperatures will peak at different wavelengths that are inversely proportional to the temperature.

name: Rayleigh-Jeans Law
author: Lord Rayleigh & James Jeans
description: An approximation to the spectral radiance of electromagnetic radiation as a function of wavelength from a black body at a given temperature.

name: Friedmann Equation
author: Alexander Friedmann
description: Governs the expansion of space in homogeneous and isotropic models of the universe.

name: Schwarzschild Radius
author: Karl Schwarzschild
description: The radius of a sphere such that, if all the mass of an object were to be compressed within that sphere, the escape velocity would equal the speed of light.

name: Bekenstein-Hawking Entropy
author: Jacob Bekenstein & Stephen Hawking
description: The amount of entropy that must be assigned to a black hole in order for it to comply with the laws of thermodynamics.

name: Gompertz Curve
author: Benjamin Gompertz
description: A type of mathematical model for a time series, where growth is slowest at the start and end of a time period.

name: Zipf's Law
author: George Kingsley Zipf
description: An empirical law formulated using mathematical statistics that refers to the fact that many types of data studied in the physical and social sciences can be approximated with a Zipfian distribution.

name: Mach Number
author: Ernst Mach
description: A dimensionless quantity representing the ratio of flow velocity past a boundary to the local speed of sound.

name: Divergence Theorem
author: Carl Friedrich Gauss
description: Relates the flux of a vector field through a closed surface to the divergence of the field in the volume enclosed.

name: Stokes' Theorem
author: George Gabriel Stokes
description: A statement about the integration of differential forms on manifolds, which simplifies and generalizes several theorems from vector calculus.

name: Green's Theorem
author: George Green
description: Relates a line integral around a simple closed curve C to a double integral over the plane region D bounded by C.
latex: \oint_C (L dx + M dy) = \iint_D \left(\frac{\partial M}{\partial x} - \frac{\partial L}{\partial y}\right) dx dy

name: Gauss-Bonnet Theorem
author: Carl Friedrich Gauss & Pierre Ossian Bonnet
description: Connects the geometry of surfaces to their topology.

name: Cauchy's Integral Formula
author: Augustin-Louis Cauchy
description: A central statement in complex analysis expressing the fact that a holomorphic function defined on a disk is completely determined by its values on the boundary.

name: Basel Problem
author: Leonhard Euler
description: A famous problem in mathematical analysis concerning the infinite sum of the reciprocals of the squares of the natural numbers.

name: Doppler Effect (Sound)
author: Christian Doppler
description: The change in frequency of a wave in relation to an observer who is moving relative to the wave source.

name: Planck's Law
author: Max Planck
description: Describes the spectral density of electromagnetic radiation emitted by a black body in thermal equilibrium at a given temperature T.

name: Saha Ionization Equation
author: Meghnad Saha
description: Relates the ionization state of an element to the temperature and pressure, crucial in astrophysics.

name: Virial Theorem
author: Rudolf Clausius
description: Relates the average over time of the total kinetic energy of a stable system to the average over time of the total potential energy.

name: Shannon-Hartley Theorem
author: Claude Shannon & Ralph Hartley
description: Tells the maximum rate at which information can be transmitted over a communications channel of a specified bandwidth in the presence of noise.

name: Hawking Radiation Temperature
author: Stephen Hawking
description: Predicts that black holes emit black-body radiation due to quantum effects near the event horizon.

name: Binet's Formula
author: Jacques Philippe Marie Binet
description: Expresses the n-th Fibonacci number explicitly as a function of the golden ratio.

name: Golden Ratio
author: Euclid
description: An irrational number often found in nature, art, and architecture, representing perfect proportionality.

name: Mandelbrot Set
author: Benoit Mandelbrot
description: The formula that generates the most famous fractal in mathematics, showing infinite complexity from simple rules.

name: Logistic Map
author: Robert May
description: A polynomial mapping that became an archetypal example of how complex, chaotic behaviour can arise from very simple non-linear dynamical equations.

name: Gamma Function
author: Leonhard Euler
description: An extension of the factorial function, with its argument shifted down by 1, to real and complex numbers.

name: Law of Cosines
author: Al-Kashi
description: Relates the lengths of the sides of a triangle to the cosine of one of its angles.

name: Bell's Inequality (CHSH)
author: John Stewart Bell
description: Proves that quantum mechanics is incompatible with local hidden-variable theories.

name: Born Rule
author: Max Born
description: Gives the probability that a measurement of a quantum system will yield a given result.

name: Clausius-Clapeyron Equation
author: Rudolf Clausius & Émile Clapeyron
description: Characterizes a discontinuous phase transition between two phases of matter of a single constituent.

name: Raoult's Law
author: François-Marie Raoult
description: States that the partial vapor pressure of each component of an ideal mixture of liquids is equal to the vapor pressure of the pure component multiplied by its mole fraction.

name: Prandtl Number
author: Ludwig Prandtl
description: A dimensionless number defining the ratio of momentum diffusivity to thermal diffusivity.

name: Langmuir Isotherm
author: Irving Langmuir
description: Relates the adsorption of molecules on a solid surface to gas pressure or concentration of a medium above the solid surface.

name: Nyquist-Shannon Sampling Theorem
author: Harry Nyquist & Claude Shannon
description: Establishes a sufficient condition for a sample rate that permits a discrete sequence of samples to capture all the information from a continuous-time signal.

name: Little's Law
author: John Little
description: A theorem in queueing theory that dictates the long-term average number of customers in a stationary system.

name: Beer-Lambert Law
author: August Beer & Johann Lambert
description: Relates the attenuation of light to the properties of the material through which the light is travelling.

name: Malus's Law
author: Étienne-Louis Malus
description: Gives the intensity of a plane-polarized light wave after passing through a polarizer.

name: Escape Velocity
author: Isaac Newton
description: The minimum speed needed for a free, non-propelled object to escape from the gravitational influence of a massive body.

name: Orbital Velocity
author: Johannes Kepler
description: The speed at which an object orbits around the barycenter of a system, usually around a much more massive body.

name: Euler-Mascheroni Constant
author: Leonhard Euler
description: A mathematical constant recurring in analysis and number theory, defined as the limiting difference between the harmonic series and the natural logarithm.

name: Continuity Equation
author: Leonhard Euler
description: Describes the transport of some conserved quantity (like mass or charge) in fluid dynamics and electromagnetism.

name: Poiseuille's Law
author: Jean Léonard Marie Poiseuille
description: Gives the pressure drop in an incompressible and Newtonian fluid in laminar flow flowing through a long cylindrical pipe.

name: Capacitance of a Parallel-Plate
author: Alessandro Volta
description: Calculates the capacitance of a standard parallel-plate capacitor based on its area and distance between plates.

name: Inductance of a Solenoid
author: André-Marie Ampère
description: Determines the inductance of a tightly wound coil of wire (solenoid) based on its physical properties.

name: Energy stored in a Capacitor
author: James Clerk Maxwell
description: Represents the electric potential energy stored within a charged capacitor.

name: Energy stored in an Inductor
author: James Clerk Maxwell
description: Represents the magnetic potential energy stored within an inductor carrying a current.

name: Lens Maker's Equation
author: Christiaan Huygens
description: Used to calculate the focal length of a lens based on its index of refraction and the radii of curvature of its surfaces.

name: Thin Lens Equation
author: Rene Descartes
description: Relates the object distance, image distance, and focal length for a thin lens.

name: Magnification Equation
author: Johannes Kepler
description: Calculates the linear magnification produced by an optical system.

name: Brunt-Väisälä Frequency
author: David Brunt & Vilho Väisälä
description: The angular frequency at which a vertically displaced parcel will oscillate within a statically stable environment.

name: Rossby Number
author: Carl-Gustaf Rossby
description: A dimensionless number used in fluid mechanics to describe fluid flow with respect to the Coriolis effect.

name: Coriolis Parameter
author: Gaspard-Gustave de Coriolis
description: Used in meteorology and oceanography, representing the local vertical component of the Earth's rotation.

name: Young-Laplace Equation
author: Thomas Young & Pierre-Simon Laplace
description: Describes the capillary pressure difference sustained across the interface between two static fluids due to surface tension.

name: Froude Number
author: William Froude
description: A dimensionless number defined as the ratio of the flow inertia to the external field (the latter in many applications simply due to gravity).

name: Grashof Number
author: Franz Grashof
description: A dimensionless number in fluid dynamics and heat transfer which approximates the ratio of the buoyancy to viscous force acting on a fluid.

name: Boussinesq Approximation
author: Joseph Boussinesq
description: Used in the field of buoyancy-driven flow, it assumes density differences are sufficiently small to be neglected, except where they appear in terms multiplied by g.

name: Archimedes' Principle
author: Archimedes of Syracuse
description: States that the upward buoyant force that is exerted on a body immersed in a fluid is equal to the weight of the fluid that the body displaces.

name: Stefan-Boltzmann Law (Energy Density)
author: Josef Stefan
description: Shows the energy density of a black body radiation gas.

name: Schrödinger Equation (Time-Independent)
author: Erwin Schrödinger
description: The eigenvalue equation for the Hamiltonian operator, used to find stationary states of a quantum system.

name: Pythagorean Trigonometric Identity
author: Pythagoras
description: An essential identity expressing the Pythagorean theorem in terms of trigonometric functions.

name: Van der Waals Equation
author: Johannes Diderik van der Waals
description: An equation of state that extends the ideal gas law to include the effects of non-zero molecular volume and intermolecular attractive forces.

name: Energy-Momentum Relation
author: Albert Einstein
description: The relativistic equation relating the total energy, invariant mass, and momentum of a particle.

name: Drag Equation
author: Lord Rayleigh
description: Calculates the force of drag experienced by an object moving through a fluid.

name: Boyle's Law
author: Robert Boyle
description: An experimental gas law that describes how the pressure of a gas tends to decrease as the volume of the container increases.

name: Charles's Law
author: Jacques Charles
description: An experimental gas law that describes how gases tend to expand when heated.

name: Avogadro's Law
author: Amedeo Avogadro
description: States that equal volumes of all gases, at the same temperature and pressure, have the same number of molecules.

name: Relativistic Momentum
author: Albert Einstein
description: The momentum of an object moving at speeds close to the speed of light.

name: Moment of Inertia (Solid Sphere)
author: Leonhard Euler
description: Calculates the rotational inertia of a uniform solid sphere about an axis through its center.

name: Parallel Axis Theorem
author: Christiaan Huygens & Jakob Steiner
description: Determines the moment of inertia of a rigid body about any axis, given its moment of inertia about a parallel axis through the center of mass.

name: Terminal Velocity
author: Isaac Newton
description: The maximum velocity attainable by an object as it falls through a fluid.

name: Torricelli's Law
author: Evangelista Torricelli
description: Relates the speed of fluid flowing from an orifice to the height of fluid above the opening.

name: Hydrostatic Pressure
author: Blaise Pascal
description: The pressure exerted by a fluid at equilibrium at a given point within the fluid, due to the force of gravity.

name: Elastic Potential Energy
author: Robert Hooke
description: The potential energy stored as a result of deformation of an elastic object, such as the stretching of a spring.

name: Joule's First Law
author: James Prescott Joule
description: Expresses the relationship between heat generated and current flowing through a conductor.

name: Hall Voltage
author: Edwin Hall
description: The voltage difference generated across an electrical conductor, transverse to an electric current in the conductor and to an applied magnetic field.

name: Dalton's Law
author: John Dalton
description: States that in a mixture of non-reacting gases, the total pressure exerted is equal to the sum of the partial pressures of the individual gases.

name: Henry's Law
author: William Henry
description: States that the amount of dissolved gas in a liquid is proportional to its partial pressure above the liquid.

name: Graham's Law
author: Thomas Graham
description: States that the rate of effusion of a gas is inversely proportional to the square root of the mass of its particles.

name: Fermat's Little Theorem
author: Pierre de Fermat
description: States that if p is a prime number, then for any integer a, the number a^p - a is an integer multiple of p. Foundational for cryptography.

name: Gaussian Curvature
author: Carl Friedrich Gauss
description: The intrinsic measure of curvature of a surface, defined as the product of its principal curvatures.

name: Cauchy-Schwarz Inequality
author: Augustin-Louis Cauchy & Hermann Schwarz
description: An elementary inequality in inner product spaces, widely used in linear algebra and analysis.

name: Triangle Inequality
author: Euclid
description: States that for any triangle, the sum of the lengths of any two sides must be greater than or equal to the length of the remaining side.

name: Chandrasekhar Limit
author: Subrahmanyan Chandrasekhar
description: The maximum mass of a stable white dwarf star. Beyond this, it will collapse into a neutron star or black hole.

name: Roche Limit
author: Édouard Roche
description: The minimum distance to which a large satellite can approach its primary body without tidal forces overcoming the satellite's internal gravity.

name: Radar Equation
author: WWII Scientists
description: Relates the expected receive power to the transmitted power, antenna gains, range, and scattering cross-section of a target.

name: Friis Transmission Equation
author: Harald T. Friis
description: Used in telecommunications engineering to calculate the power received from one antenna (with ideal line-of-sight) to another.

name: Casimir Effect Force
author: Hendrik Casimir
description: Calculates the attractive force between two uncharged conducting plates in a vacuum, caused by quantum vacuum fluctuations.

name: Handshaking Lemma
author: Leonhard Euler
description: A statement in graph theory: every finite undirected graph has an even number of vertices with odd degree.

name: Kepler's Second Law
author: Johannes Kepler
description: A line segment joining a planet and the Sun sweeps out equal areas during equal intervals of time.

name: Specific Heat Equation
author: Joseph Black
description: Calculates the heat energy required to change the temperature of a substance.

name: Coulomb's Law of Friction
author: Charles-Augustin de Coulomb
description: A model used to calculate the force of dry friction.

name: Amdahl's Law
author: Gene Amdahl
description: Used in computer science to find the maximum expected improvement to an overall system when only part of the system is improved.

name: Moore's Law
author: Gordon Moore
description: An observation that the number of transistors in a dense integrated circuit doubles about every two years.

name: Ehrenfest Theorem
author: Paul Ehrenfest
description: Relates the time derivative of the expectation values of the position and momentum operators to the expectation value of the force.

name: Larmor Precession
author: Joseph Larmor
description: Calculates the frequency of precession of the magnetic moment of an object about an external magnetic field.

name: Moseley's Law
author: Henry Moseley
description: An empirical law concerning the characteristic x-rays emitted by atoms, directly linking to the atomic number.

name: Euler Equations (Fluid Dynamics)
author: Leonhard Euler
description: A set of quasilinear hyperbolic equations governing adiabatic and inviscid flow.

name: Heron's Formula
author: Hero of Alexandria
description: Calculates the area of a triangle given the lengths of all three sides.

name: Law of Sines
author: Nasir al-Din al-Tusi
description: An equation relating the lengths of the sides of a triangle to the sines of its angles.

name: Volume of an n-ball
author: Hermann Weyl
description: The formula for the volume of an n-dimensional sphere of radius R.

name: Nash Equilibrium
author: John Forbes Nash Jr.
description: A concept within game theory where the optimal outcome of a game is one where no player has an incentive to deviate from their chosen strategy after considering an opponent's choice.

name: Gross-Pitaevskii Equation
author: Eugene Gross & Lev Pitaevskii
description: Describes the ground state of a quantum system of identical bosons using the Hartree-Fock approximation and the pseudopotential interaction model.

name: London Equation
author: Fritz & Heinz London
description: Relates the current to the electromagnetic field in and around a superconductor, explaining the Meissner effect.

name: Drude Model for Electrical Conductivity
author: Paul Drude
description: Explains the transport properties of electrons in materials, especially metals, using kinetic theory.

name: Bloch's Theorem
author: Felix Bloch
description: Describes the energy eigenstates for an electron in a crystal lattice.

name: Shannon's Source Coding Theorem
author: Claude Shannon
description: Establishes the limits to possible data compression, and the operational meaning of the Shannon entropy.

name: Gini Coefficient
author: Corrado Gini
description: A measure of statistical dispersion intended to represent the income inequality or wealth inequality within a nation or any other group of people.

name: Okun's Law
author: Arthur Okun
description: An empirically observed relationship between unemployment and losses in a country's production.

name: Solow-Swan Model
author: Robert Solow & Trevor Swan
description: An economic model of long-run economic growth set within the framework of neoclassical economics.

name: Taylor Rule
author: John B. Taylor
description: A monetary policy targeting rule that stipulates how much the central bank should change the nominal interest rate in response to changes in inflation.

name: Euler's Totient Theorem
author: Leonhard Euler
description: A fundamental theorem in number theory, generalizing Fermat's Little Theorem, heavily used in RSA encryption.

name: Wilson's Theorem
author: John Wilson

name: Wallis Product for Pi

name: Theorema Egregium

name: Cauchy Momentum Equation

name: Korteweg-De Vries Equation

name: Abbe Resolution Limit

name: Heaviside Step Function

name: Nusselt Number

author: Lorenzo A. Richards
description: The fundamental governing equation of hydrology representing the movement of water in unsaturated soils.

name: Gumbel Distribution (CDF)
author: Emil Julius Gumbel
description: Used in extreme value theory to model the distribution of the maximum (or minimum) of a number of samples. Used to predict 100-year floods or extreme earthquakes.

name: Pareto Distribution
author: Vilfredo Pareto
description: The mathematical formulation of the '80/20 rule', describing the unequal distribution of wealth, city sizes, and file sizes on the internet.

name: Metcalfe's Law
author: Robert Metcalfe
description: States that the value of a telecommunications network is proportional to the square of the number of connected users of the system.

name: H-index Formula
author: Jorge E. Hirsch
description: An author-level metric that attempts to measure both the productivity and citation impact of the publications of a scientist or scholar.

name: Cardano's Formula
author: Gerolamo Cardano
description: The exact algebraic solution for the roots of a depressed cubic equation.

name: Schwarzschild Metric
author: Karl Schwarzschild
description: The exact solution to Einstein's field equations that describes the gravitational field outside a spherical, non-rotating mass like a star or black hole.
latex: ds^2 = -\left(1 - \frac{r_s}{r}\right) c^2 dt^2 + \left(1 - \frac{r_s}{r}\right)^{-1} dr^2 + r^2 d\Omega^2

name: Malthusian Growth Model
author: Thomas Robert Malthus
description: The original exponential growth formula used to project population growth under the assumption of infinite resources.

name: Gompertz Curve for Tumor Growth
author: Benjamin Gompertz
description: Adapted for oncology, it models how the growth rate of a tumor slows down as the tumor mass gets larger due to lack of nutrients.

name: Lotka's Law
author: Alfred J. Lotka
description: Describes the frequency of publication by authors in any given field, stating that the number of authors making n contributions is about 1/n^2 of those making one.

name: Drake Equation (Updated for Bio-signatures)
author: Sara Seager
description: A parallel to the original Drake equation, focusing on the search for life by detecting biosignature gases on exoplanets.
latex: N = N^* F_q F_{HZ} F_o F_L F_s

name: The Equation of Love
author: Hannah Fry
description: A mathematical model based on the Gottman-Murray equations, predicting how much a couple will bicker or stay together based on their mood and influence.

name: Procrastination Equation
author: Piers Steel
description: A mathematical model of motivation that explains why we delay tasks based on expectancy, value, impulsiveness, and delay.

name: Bubble Lifetime in Beer
author: Gérard Liger-Belair
description: Calculates the growth of bubbles in carbonated beverages like champagne or beer as they rise to the surface.

name: The Perfect Sandwich Formula
author: Mark Hadley
description: An actual formula commissioned to find the mathematical balance of bread, filling, and condiments.

name: Nonlinear Schrödinger Equation (Rogue Waves)
author: Akira Hasegawa
description: Used to model 'Rogue Waves'—massive, spontaneous ocean waves that can sink large ships.

name: Kardashev Scale (Energy Consumption)
author: Nikolai Kardashev
description: A method of measuring a civilization's level of technological advancement based on the amount of energy they can use.

name: Schwartzman Paradox (Global Warming)
author: David Schwartzman
description: An equation relating the biological cooling of Earth to the weathering of silicate rocks.

name: The Speed of a Rumor
author: Daley & Kendall
description: A mathematical model of rumor spreading in a population, similar to an epidemic model.

name: Coffee Ring Effect
author: Robert Deegan
description: The fluid dynamics equation explaining why coffee stains are darker at the edges than in the middle.

name: Heat Death of the Universe (Entropy Max)
author: Lord Kelvin
description: The thermodynamic limit where the universe reaches a state of maximum entropy and no more work can be performed.

name: Moore's Law of Space (Rocket Launch Costs)
author: Space Industry Standard
description: Models the exponential decrease in the cost per kilogram to send payloads into Low Earth Orbit (LEO).

name: Zipf's Law for City Sizes
author: George Zipf
description: Observes that the population of a city is inversely proportional to its rank in the list of cities in a country.

name: Complexity of a Rubik's Cube
author: David Singmaster
description: The formula calculating the total number of possible permutations for a standard 3x3x3 Rubik's Cube.

name: The Law of Rent
author: David Ricardo
description: States that the rent of a land site is equal to the economic advantage obtained by using the site in its most productive use.

name: Ideal Rocket Velocity (with Gravity)
author: Konstantin Tsiolkovsky
description: An extension of the rocket equation that accounts for gravity losses during the climb.

name: Fitts's Law (HCI)
author: Paul Fitts
description: Predicts the time required to rapidly move to a target area, a staple in User Interface (UI) design.

name: Hick's Law (HCI)
author: William Edmund Hick
description: Describes the time it takes for a person to make a decision as a result of the possible choices they have.

name: Price Elasticity of Demand
author: Alfred Marshall
description: Measures the responsiveness of the quantity demanded of a good to a change in its price.

name: Veblen Good Demand
author: Thorstein Veblen
description: An anomaly where the demand for a luxury good increases as the price increases, contradicting standard economic laws.

name: Gresham's Law
author: Sir Thomas Gresham
description: The principle that 'bad money drives out good' in a system where two types of currency are in circulation.

name: Benoulli's Principle for Sails
author: Daniel Bernoulli
description: Explains how a sailboat can travel faster than the wind by creating a pressure difference across the sail.

name: The Law of Diminishing Returns
author: Anne Robert Jacques Turgot
description: States that if one input in the production of a commodity is increased while others are fixed, the marginal output will eventually decrease.

name: Metabolic Theory of Ecology
author: James Brown
description: Predicts how the metabolic rate of organisms governs ecological processes from individuals to ecosystems.

name: The Weaire-Phelan Structure (Foam)
author: Denis Weaire & Robert Phelan
description: The most efficient way to partition space into cells of equal volume with the least surface area (the geometry of bubbles).

name: Universal Law of Generalization
author: Roger Shepard
description: A fundamental law of psychology stating that the probability of a response generalizes from one stimulus to another as an exponential function of distance in psychological space.

name: Penrose Process (Black Hole Energy)
author: Roger Penrose
description: Describes how energy can be extracted from a rotating black hole by tossing an object into its ergosphere.

name: Hawking-Hartle State (No Boundary)
author: Stephen Hawking & James Hartle
description: A proposal in quantum cosmology that the universe has no boundaries in imaginary time, implying it had no beginning.

name: The Bekenstein-Hawking Entropy (Full Form)
author: Stephen Hawking
description: The complete version of the entropy of a black hole, linking thermodynamics, gravity, and quantum mechanics.

name: Kerr-Newman Metric
author: Ezra Newman
description: The most general solution for a stationary, rotating, electrically charged black hole.

name: The 'Wait-Time' Paradox
author: Probability Standard
description: The mathematical reason why the bus always seems to take longer than the average waiting time.

name: Oklo Natural Reactor Power
author: Francis Perrin
description: The formula used to calculate the power output of the only known natural nuclear fission reactor on Earth (in Gabon).

name: The Drake Equation for Earth Killers
author: Impact Research
description: Calculates the probability of a major asteroid impact capable of causing mass extinction.

name: The Leidenfrost Effect Time
author: Johann Gottlob Leidenfrost
description: Predicts the lifetime of a water droplet floating on a cushion of its own vapor on a very hot surface.

name: The Tsiolkovsky Equation for Interstellar Travel
author: Relativistic Rocketry
description: The relativistic version of the rocket equation for travel at significant fractions of the speed of light.

name: The Shannon Entropy of English
author: Claude Shannon
description: Calculates the information density of the English language (about 0.6 to 1.3 bits per character).

name: The Maximum Human Lifespan Formula
author: Gompertz-Makeham
description: Predicts the probability of death at a given age, suggesting a theoretical biological limit for humans.

name: The Stability of the Solar System
author: Jacques Laskar
description: Calculates the Lyapunov time of the solar system, showing it is chaotic with a horizon of 5 million years.

name: The Logistic Map (Bifurcation Point)
author: Mitchell Feigenbaum
description: The constant that describes the rate at which a system approaches chaos.

name: The Final Equation of the Universe (Friedmann)
author: Alexander Friedmann
description: Determines the ultimate fate of the universe: Big Freeze, Big Crunch, or Big Rip.
